title: oxalis × vanaelstii
intro: Oxalis × vanaelstii, also known as the Van Aelst's sorrel or wood sorrel, is a rare hybrid species within the Oxalis genus of flowering plants. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Oxalis × vanaelstii include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Oxalis × vanaelstii can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its usage should be carefully considered due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
